mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market driving consumer spending and corporate earnings, which pushes equity prices higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: A rolling 12-month correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 20-day returns of less than 0.1, or a Sharpe ratio of the long position less than 0.3 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises to 4.5% or above, it signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% and remains above for at least two months.

mechanism_narrative: The conjunction of a high federal funds rate (above 5%) and declining housing starts (below 1700) indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment and housing sector weakness, which together depress economic growth and stock prices.
failure_condition: The spread in average forward return between joint-triggered and non-triggered periods is less than 100 bps over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation (CPI above 200), further increases in the CPIAUCSL trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: No negative average forward return in months following CPI increases when CPI is above 200, or p-value of one-sided t-test > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity and overall economic output, which supports higher corporate profits and stock market returns over a 3-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 forward returns is negative or zero over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS above 360B) and payroll employment (PAYEMS above 136M) signals robust consumer-driven economic expansion, leading to positive stock returns.
failure_condition: In instances where both triggers are met, forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of events.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 1700 units per month signals a contraction in the housing market, which historically precedes consumer spending cuts and equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following housing starts below 1700 is positive in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, defined as the federal funds rate above 5% and rising, valuation compression from higher discount rates dominates, producing negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Average forward 20-day return is positive in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 5% and the 3-month change is positive.
